parts.title: Biology Teachers
prompts: Create a lesson plan for a biology lesson in grade [X] on [specific theme, e.g. evolution], with three phases focusing on theory, observation, and analysis, and a connection to the national curriculum.
Give me five strategies for teaching grade [X] students about [specific theme, e.g. cells], with a practical exercise for each and a reflection on its usefulness.
Write a short guide for introducing [specific theme, e.g. ecosystems] to a grade [X] class, with three steps and an engaging field activity.
Suggest three ways to use digital tools to teach [specific theme, e.g. genetics] in grade [X], with a simulation for each and a connection to digital competence in the national curriculum.
Create a list of five experimental tasks for a grade [X] class on [specific theme, e.g. photosynthesis], with a hypothesis and analysis question for each.
Give me an example of a routine for starting a biology lesson in grade [X] on [specific theme, e.g. the human body], with three steps and a connection to everyday phenomena.
Suggest three ways to integrate [specific theme, e.g. ecology] in a grade [X] class with practical exercises, with a field experiment for each and a reflection.
Create an assessment rubric for a biology lesson in grade [X] on [specific theme, e.g. evolution], with three knowledge requirements from the national curriculum and criteria for different levels.
Give me five ideas for teaching [specific theme, e.g. cells] in grade [X], with a creative activity for each and a connection to biological processes.
Write a short template for planning a biology lesson in grade [X] on [specific theme, e.g. genetics], with three phases (introduction, experiment, reflection) and a problem-solving task.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with a [chemistry teacher] on a cross-curricular project about [specific theme, e.g. biochemistry] in grade [X], with one idea for each and a learning goal.
Give me an example of a lesson in grade [X] on [specific theme, e.g. ecosystems], with three steps focusing on observation and an analysis task.
Create a list of five discussion questions for a grade [X] class on [specific theme, e.g. sustainability], focusing on biological connections and a link to current issues.
Suggest three ways to use everyday examples to teach [specific theme, e.g. the human body] in grade [X], with a demonstration for each and an analysis of the phenomenon.
Write a short guide for teaching [specific theme, e.g. photosynthesis] in grade [X], with three steps and a connection to scientific method as outlined in the national curriculum.
Give me five ideas for making [specific theme, e.g. evolution] more engaging for a grade 9 class, with a practical activity for each and a rationale.
Suggest three ways to assess students' understanding of [specific theme, e.g. ecology] in grade [X], with one method for each and a connection to the national curriculum's knowledge requirements.
Create a plan for a biology lesson in grade [X] on [specific theme, e.g. cells], with three phases and a closing reflection task on biological processes.
Give me an example of an experimental exercise for a grade [X] class on [specific theme, e.g. genetics], with three steps and an analysis of results.
Write a list of three short ways to connect [specific theme, e.g. ecosystems] to students' everyday lives in a grade [X] class, with a question for each and a connection to the national curriculum.
Create a detailed plan for a project on [specific theme, e.g. sustainability] for a grade [X] class, with three lessons focusing on biological processes and solutions.
Give me five strategies for teaching [specific theme, e.g. the human body] in grade [X], with an experimental activity for each and a connection to biological connections.
Write a short guide for using fieldwork in a biology lesson on [specific theme, e.g. ecology] in grade [X], with three steps and an analysis task.
Suggest three ways to integrate [specific theme, e.g. evolution] with geography in a grade [X] class, with a cross-curricular activity for each and a learning goal.
Create a list of five creative problem-solving tasks for a grade [X] class on [specific theme, e.g. photosynthesis], with instructions for each and a connection to everyday phenomena.
Give me an example of a routine for closing a biology lesson on [specific theme, e.g. genetics] in grade [X], with three steps and a reflection question.
Suggest three ways to use experiments to teach [specific theme, e.g. ecosystems] in grade [X], with a lab activity for each and an analysis of results.
